name: Never Fail Dumplings
recipeInstructions:
1. Beat the egg well, then add 6 tablespoons of cold water. Measure the water carefully.
2. Add oil and salt to the mixture; whisk together.
3. Mix baking powder and flour separately, then blend the two mixtures into a smooth batter quickly.
4. Drop dumplings into boiling stew and cover. Continue boiling for 15–20 minutes.
5. Resist the urge to peek too often—dumplings will rise and produce a light, tender crust. A cold draught may cause them to fall.
6. They can also be served as dessert by cooking in boiling water and serving with pancake syrup or jam drizzled over them.
These dumplings were popular camp desserts for young scouts during wartime due to readily available ingredients from limited home rations, often called "Sinkers." When traveling, the dry ingredients are prepared in batches and stored in zip-lock bags, ready to add liquid and egg when used.
